description: The Tanglewood Boys was an Italian-American recruitment gang or farm team for the Lucchese crime family, operating from the Tanglewood Shopping Center in Yonkers, New York. Active from the 1990s to the 2000s, the gang was involved in murders, assaults, armed robbery, arsons, and bookmaking in Westchester County, the Bronx, and the Upper West Side. Many members were sons of made men. The gang's downfall followed high-profile murders and subsequent prosecutions.
